image-generation.prompt_string: Generate image of an abandoned coastal city after storm, golden hour low tide wrecked boats on shore, driftwood and barnacles, collapsed boardwalk and ruined buildings, sunset horizon with storm clouds, wide panoramic leading lines from shore to horizon, cinematic landscape, Roger Deakins lighting style, photoreal landscape render, ultra wide 6k, golden rim light low sun from right warm soft, warm gold slate blue muted green palette, wide angle low vantage 24mm lens panoramic, high detail wood grain peeling paint water ripples, volumetric light spray particles subtle lens flare, melancholic serene mood post storm clearing, props broken street lamp rusted sign

context.value: golden hour, low tide, wrecked boats on shore

composition.spatial_layout: wide panoramic, leading lines from shore to horizon

color.palette: warm gold, slate blue, muted green

detail.micro_detail: wood grain, peeling paint, water ripples

image-generation.negative_prompt: people, vehicles, modern signage, text overlay, high saturation, toy like

programming.prompt_string: Write a Python project that implements a sentiment analysis bot. Provide a training script train.py that by default builds a scikit learn pipeline using TfidfVectorizer and LogisticRegression and that can optionally fine tune a transformer when passed model_type=transformer. Save the trained model and preprocessing pipeline to disk. Implement a FastAPI app main.py exposing POST /predict that accepts {text: string} or {texts: [string]} and returns {label: "positive|neutral|negative", score: float, model: string}. Include a CLI run.py with a predict command matching the API. Add unit tests and integration tests using pytest including tests for invalid input. Include logging, input validation, batch predict, and a README with instructions to train run and evaluate. Keep code PEP8 compliant with type hints, and include brief comments explaining design choices and trade offs.
programming.goal: Create a production ready Python service that classifies text into positive neutral or negative and can be used interactively via an API and as a simple CLI tool
requirements: Provide a training script that trains a classifier using either a scikit learn pipeline or a fine tuned transformer model depending on a config flag
Expose a FastAPI endpoint POST /predict that accepts JSON {"text": "..."} and returns {"label": "positive|neutral|negative","score": float}
Provide a CLI command `python run.py predict --text "..."` that returns the same response as the API
Persist the trained model and preprocessing pipeline to disk using joblib for scikit learn or torch save for transformers
Include a lightweight tokenizer and consistent preprocessing steps such as lowercasing trimming and stopword handling configurable via config file
Support batch predictions for arrays of texts
Include unit tests and one integration test that runs the FastAPI app in test client mode
Log requests and predictions with levels info and error using python logging, but do not log raw sensitive content
Provide basic input validation and return 400 for invalid input

constraints.style: Follow PEP8 and use type hints. Keep functions small and single responsibility. No Lombok style magic. Use clear docstrings.
constraints.complexity: Keep training and inference code readable. Default pipeline should use scikit learn with TF IDF and a linear model. Transformer option may be included but must be switchable and optional.
constraints.performance: Provide a batch predict method that is memory conscious and uses vectorized operations. For scikit learn pipeline ensure vectorizer and model are memory efficient.
constraints.memory: Avoid loading the model more than once per process. Use lazy loading on first request.
constraints.security: Sanitize inputs for length and deny extremely large payloads. Do not execute untrusted code. Do not store user text unless opt in.

input_spec.types: JSON body with key text or array of texts
input_spec.size_limits: Single text max 5000 characters. Batch size max 256 items.

edge_cases: very long text near 5000 char limit
deliverables: train.py training script with config options
model file model.joblib or model.pt and preprocessing pipeline
api app main.py with FastAPI and endpoints
README with run instructions, example requests, evaluation metrics and how to switch between scikit learn and transformer mode
negative_constraints: Do not include large dataset files in the repo
Do not use global mutable state for model storage
Do not leak training data or user inputs in logs

planning.prompt_string: You are a professional travel planner. Create a complete 14 day exploration itinerary for a trip across North Africa covering Morocco Tunisia and Egypt. Include a summary, day by day timeline with time windows, intercity and intracountry transport recommendations, desert segment planning, accommodation suggestions, cultural and safety guidance, packing list, booking checklist, budget range, risk analysis and mitigations, and alternatives for lower budget or slower pace. Avoid generic filler and avoid unrealistic assumptions about live prices or unsafe locations. Keep output structured and actionable.
planning.objective: Plan a 2 week exploration trip across North Africa focusing on culture history food nature and local experiences. Produce a complete itinerary with logistics budgets and fallback options.

soft_limits: prefer historic sites and natural scenery

special_requirements: some days must allow flexible downtime

known_items: entry points may include Casablanca Tunis Cairo

deliverables: 14 day itinerary with times travel windows and key activities
transport plan for intercity and intracountry travel
alternatives for slower pacing and lower budget
checklist for bookings and pre trip tasks

detailed_plan.instructions: choose most efficient routing from origin to North Africa

risks_and_mitigations.risk: sandstorms or extreme heat during desert segments
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: schedule desert day early morning, carry water and cover gear

lower_budget: use trains and shared taxis where safe
stay in riads or guesthouses rather than hotels
reduce domestic flights by choosing two countries instead of three
higher_budget: stay in boutique hotels and desert lodges
time_constrained: skip Egypt or Tunisia to avoid rushed travel

negative_prompt: avoid assuming exact real time prices
avoid unrealistic cross country travel times
